dict.string: This utility will optimize your Apple Mail app's sqlite3 envelope database to help Mail operate more efficiently. It is based on the bash script by Paul Galow https://github.com/pbihq/tools which inspired by Brett Terpstra's post http://brettterpstra.com/2015/10/27/vacuuming-mail-dot-app-on-el-capitan/
This will will Quit your Mail app and then optimize its sqlite3 envelope database . Click "OK" to continue, or click "Cancel" to exit.

#!/bin/bash # Optimize Apple Mail database (Envelope Index vacuum) # Converted from AppleScript by Alex Narvey / Precursor Systems # For use as a Munki on-demand install script
# Get the current logged-in user CURRENT_USER=$(stat -f "%Su" /dev/console) USER_HOME=$(dscl . -read /Users/"$CURRENT_USER" NFSHomeDirectory | awk '{print $2}')
# Notify user osascript -e 'display dialog "Apple Mail will be quit and an optimization process will occur which will take a minute or two. Please be patient and wait for the next dialog box." buttons {"OK"} default button "OK"'
# Quit Mail if running if pgrep -x "Mail" > /dev/null; then osascript -e 'tell application "Mail" to quit' sleep 5 fi
# Path to Mail Envelope Index (V10 = macOS 13+) DB_PATH="$USER_HOME/Library/Mail/V10/MailData/Envelope Index"
if [ ! -f "$DB_PATH" ]; then osascript -e 'display dialog "Could not locate the Mail Envelope Index. Aborting." buttons {"OK"} default button "OK"' exit 1 fi
# Get size before vacuum SIZE_BEFORE=$(stat -f%z "$DB_PATH")
# Vacuum the database /usr/bin/sqlite3 "$DB_PATH" 'VACUUM;' VACUUM_RESULT=$?
#if [ $VACUUM_RESULT -ne 0 ]; then # osascript -e 'display dialog "The optimization failed. The Mail database may be in use or corrupted." buttons {"OK"} default button "OK"' # exit 1 #fi
# Get size after vacuum SIZE_AFTER=$(stat -f%z "$DB_PATH")
# Convert to MB (rounded) SIZE_BEFORE_MB=$(echo "scale=0; $SIZE_BEFORE / 1000000" | bc) SIZE_AFTER_MB=$(echo "scale=0; $SIZE_AFTER / 1000000" | bc)
# Notify user of results osascript -e "display dialog \"Mail index before: ${SIZE_BEFORE_MB} MB Mail index after: ${SIZE_AFTER_MB} MB
Your index has been optimized and Apple Mail will now launch.\" buttons {\"OK\"} default button \"OK\""
# Relaunch Mail as the current user /bin/launchctl asuser "$(id -u "$CURRENT_USER")" /usr/bin/open -a Mail
exit 0
